title: EDITORS;

Jingtong Sun

title: AI RELIABILITY REVIEW
text:
post1 notes

consider providing gentler definitions for

prompt injections / jailbreaks; not everyone knows what these terms mean, so good to define them clearly at the start.
“system level control”: which is a nuanced new term

title could be more user friendly:

the term “privilege erosion” isn’t the most clear for a general audience. For me the theme is that agents bring a new era of unmitigable set of risks.
“system control” is suggeestive of “loss of control” / XAI terms, which we may not want to evoke

love the examples; no harm in McDonald’s answering coding questions
Alignment is never guaranteed

Additional context can change condition therefore the distribution; the space is large as you argue, so guarantees with reasonable bounds are not possible. Very clear.
Great to have all the citations on the side + diagrams are so good!
“Alignment can act on a finite set of example?”: can training a finite set of examples affect the entire distribution? This is worth explaining more.

Presumably if the context necessary to shift the conditional distribution towards undesired outputs is in the “nooks and cranies” would it not also be hard for an adversary to find it?
what about gating the model outputs? That is a finite set we can more easily assess and is perhaps closer to what we propose


I like the point about no separation between data and control in LLMs, a new paradigm that’s especially challenging compared to traditional systems. I find this argument and conclusion very compelling.
Privilege erosion:

nice to end on this as a broader consequence
what about instruction hierarchy? would that not encourage the model to prioritize system instructions over user inputs?

nitpicks: “a moment at the moment”, System-level implications needs a space to render properly
